name: Kaluga Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: courage, battle and labour, traditional Russian heraldic colour
purity and the central narrow stripe
hope, youth, nature, fertility, and eternal life; taken from the oblast's coat of arms

description: Three horizontal stripes - a wide red top, a narrow white middle, and a wide green bottom - with a golden imperial crown set in the upper hoist on the red field.
history: Adopted on 30 January 2004 by the Legislative Assembly of Kaluga Oblast.

features.symbolism: the green is taken from the oblast's coat of arms and stands for hope, youth, nature, fertility, and eternal life.

features.role: imperial crown from the oblast's coat of arms
features.symbolism: a golden imperial crown set within the red stripe, taken from the Kaluga Oblast coat of arms.
